scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector uses Mechanism (ii): inspector-side tagging. Every finding includes introduced_in_diff: bool. Only findings with introduced_in_diff: true are eligible to block close per AC-2.6(b). Files outside commands/*.md and agents/*.md are classified as out-of-scope for verbosity detection; findings on such files are advisory only.

summary.cleanliness_gate_result: PASS — zero introduced_in_diff violations detected. All three changed files are outside commands/*.md and agents/*.md verbosity scope.
summary.verdict: CLEAN — no verbosity violations in this diff. All changed files are outside the commands/*.md / agents/*.md verbosity scope. No introduced_in_diff: true findings exist; close is not blocked by this inspector.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh reasoning) confirmed the draft empty findings list is correct. All three --changed-files entries are outside prompt-inspector's command/agent Markdown scope, so zero verbosity violations should be reported. One OBSERVATION_ONLY item raised (no PROPOSED_FIX): the rationale should avoid saying 'all files tagged introduced_in_diff: true' because introduced_in_diff applies to emitted findings and there are no findings to tag. This is a wording observation only, not a blocker.

findings.issue: Rationale wording 'all files tagged introduced_in_diff: true' is imprecise — introduced_in_diff is a field on finding objects, and there are no findings to tag.
findings.rationale: Codex marked this OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX. The report has been written without the imprecise wording — no 'introduced_in_diff: true' tag is applied to file-level entries when there are zero findings. Observation logged only; does not block close.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Wording corrected: report does not claim 'all files tagged introduced_in_diff: true' at the file level. The introduced_in_diff field is absent from this report's findings array (which is empty). No other changes required.
